chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. S – 64 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on CMA No.217/2024 (Ex.A) 2. For hearing of main case 24.03.2025 Mr. Ubedullah Ghoto, Advocate for the Petitioner Agha Ather Hussain Pathan, Assistant AG Sindh along with SIP Mir Hassan Bullo, SHO Police Station, Katcho Bindi-I. >>>>>..<<<<< Through instant Petition, the petitioner has prayed that the official respondents may be directed to arrest the absconding accused Mouledino involved in Crime No.08 of 2022 of Police Station, Katcho Bindi-I, District Ghotki for offence under Sections 302, 324, 147, 149, 114 and 504 PPC. The notices of the petition were issued to the official respondents. Today a statement has been filed by respondent No.6, wherein he submits that in all seven accused persons were nominated in the FIR. Out of whom six accused have been arrested by the police and reported to the trial Court for prosecution in accordance with the law. He further submits that learned trial Court has issued proclamation u/s 87 and 88 Cr.P.C against the absconding accused, wherein CNICs of all the accused have been blocked by NADRA and the immoveable properties under the title of the accused have been attached. It appears that the respondents have made sufficient efforts to seek arrest of the absconding accused Mouledino. Since the accused has shifted away to some unknown place, therefore, his arrest is not being made. The official respondents and in particular the respondents No.5 and 6 are directed to conduct regular search, if the warrants of arrest of the accused are issued by the trial Court to effect his arrest and produce him before the trial Court.

Since the accused has shifted away to some unknown place, therefore, his arrest is not being made. The official respondents and in particular the respondents No.5 and 6 are directed to conduct regular search, if the warrants of arrest of the accused are issued by the trial Court to effect his arrest and produce him before the trial Court. The petition has served its purpose and is disposed of accordingly along with pending application. Judge ARBROHI
